title: Boat Rental in Nice: 2026 Guide to Costs, Seasons & Top Yachts
html:
From the quayside at Quai des États-Unis, a bareboat charter on the French Riviera starts at around €350 for a half-day voyage to Villefranche-sur-Mer. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners so you can compare verified boat listings and secure your ideal craft on the Mediterranean.

Where to set off in Nice

The main point of departure is Port de Nice, a sheltered harbour within walking distance of the old town. From there you can motor east to the bay of Villefranche-sur-Mer, anchor off Cap Ferrat, or cruise across to the Lérins Islands off Cannes. Further west, Port Grimaud and the gulf of Saint-Tropez offer longer passages. For a quick escape, head to the Île Sainte-Marguerite, just 6 nautical miles south-west, where the crystal water invites a swim stop. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Axopar 28, best for day trips from Port de Nice to Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat; seats 8; from €350 half-day.
  2. Lagoon 42 catamaran, ideal for a relaxed full-day cruise to the Lérins Islands; sleeps 8; from €900 full-day.
  3. Princess F70, a luxurious flybridge motor yacht for a crewed charter to Monaco; accommodates 12; from €2,500 day. Check the Princess F70 for hire on BoatHire24.
  4. Sunseeker Manhattan 68, perfect for a corporate or group outing from Nice to Antibes; capacity 14; from €3,000 day.
  5. Azimut Atlantis 43, sporty open cruiser for couples exploring the Bay of Angels; seats 6; from €600 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Nice?

Boat rental in Nice ranges from €80 per person for a shared tour to €5,000 per day for a crewed superyacht. The price depends on boat size, season, and duration. Below is a typical cost band breakdown: One option worth a look is the Princess F70 for hire.

  • Shared tour (RIB or small motorboat): from €80 per person for a 2-hour cruise along the Baie des Anges.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, no licence needed): from €350 for an Axopar or similar day boat.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, skipper included): from €900 for a Lagoon 42 with up to 12 guests.
  • Crewed yacht full-day (8 hours, all inclusive): from €2,500 for a Princess or Sunseeker flybridge.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Nice?

In France, a boating licence is required for any power vessel over 6 metres or with an engine over 4.5 kW, but the operator confirms exact requirements. Many rental boats in Nice are under that threshold or include a skipper, so no personal licence is needed. In high season, May through September, the Mistral wind can whip up the sea, so a local skipper is recommended for comfort and safety. A self-contained local tip:

When the Mistral blows, the bay of Villefranche offers the most protected anchorage; head there for a calm lunch stop.
If you own a boat, you can rent out your boat and earn from spare days.

At a glance

  • Price range: €80, €5,000 per booking
  • Peak months: June, July, August
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to 8 hours (half-day or full-day)
  • Maximum guests: up to 14 on a large motor yacht
  • Currency: Euro (EUR)
  • Departure port: Port de Nice

Questions about boat rental in Nice

Can you rent a boat without a licence in Nice?

Yes, many boats under 6 metres or those with a skipper do not require the renter to hold a licence, and the platform confirms this for each listing.

What is the best month for calm sea conditions?

May, June and September offer the calmest waters, while July and August can be busy but still manageable with a morning departure.

How many people can a typical catamaran accommodate?

A standard 42-foot catamaran like the Lagoon 42 can take up to 12 guests for a day charter.

What is included in a crewed yacht charter?

A crewed charter typically includes a captain, fuel, insurance, and often snacks or a light meal, though full catering costs extra.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Nice or list your own. For owners, rent out your boat and earn money when you are not using it. Before booking, check the booking FAQ for details on deposits and cancellation policies. For any trip on the Côte d'Azur in 2026, the platform makes finding a boat straightforward.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Nice and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
